skill.name: tailwind
skill.description: Guides Tailwind CSS v4.2 browser-runtime usage for HyperFrames compositions, including utility classes, CSS-first theme tokens, validation, and v3-to-v4 migration checks.
skill.summary: Use Tailwind CSS v4.2 safely and predictably in HyperFrames composition HTML.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks, command examples, URLs, and blocker patterns, but review found documentation text rather than executable code. No prompt injection, credential access, hidden network request, or malicious behavior was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown inline code, HTML/CSS/JavaScript examples, and documented HyperFrames validation commands. SKILL.md contains no script file, hook, or automatic command execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is visibly Markdown documentation and fenced examples. The file tree contains only SKILL.md, so there is no executable implementation behind these examples.

low_findings.title: Static Network URL Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Tailwind CSS documentation and release notes. They are reference links, not code that sends data or performs network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs are public Tailwind documentation references in the credits section. No surrounding code reads secrets or transmits workspace data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert at line 3 does not correspond to cryptographic code. The line is the skill description and mentions HyperFrames and Tailwind usage only.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter prose, not code. No hash, cipher, digest, password handling, or cryptographic API appears there.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts occur on layout guidance and CSS variant text. They do not inspect the host, enumerate files, collect environment data, or invoke operating system tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Tailwind guidance and class attributes. There is no command, API call, or instruction to probe the local system.

content.value_statement: Tailwind v4 browser-runtime behavior can differ from older Tailwind projects. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce stable HyperFrames composition styles.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Tailwind CSS v4 browser runtime in HyperFrames compositions.
Shows CSS-first theme token and custom utility patterns for composition HTML.
Identifies Tailwind v3 patterns that should not be used in v4 runtime compositions.
Guides stable class usage for deterministic video frame rendering.
Provides validation and render proof commands for HyperFrames projects.
Helps debug missing styles caused by runtime readiness or dynamic class names.
limitations: It is specific to HyperFrames composition HTML and Tailwind v4 browser runtime.
It does not replace the Tailwind documentation for general CSS framework usage.
It does not manage Tailwind v3 configuration inside packages/studio.
It gives guidance only; users must run validation in their own project.

use_cases.title: Create Render-Ready Composition Styles
use_cases.description: Apply Tailwind utility classes and stable dimensions to HyperFrames scenes that must render consistently frame by frame.

use_cases.title: Migrate v3 Patterns To v4 Runtime
use_cases.description: Replace tailwind.config.js and @tailwind directive habits with v4 CSS-first theme tokens and utilities.

use_cases.title: Debug Missing Tailwind Styles
use_cases.description: Check runtime readiness, dynamic class names, and validation output when preview styles do not match rendered video.

prompt_templates.title: Add Tailwind Styling
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task for a new HyperFrames composition
prompt_templates.prompt: Style this HyperFrames composition with Tailwind v4 browser-runtime classes. Keep dimensions stable and avoid Tailwind v3 setup patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Create Theme Tokens
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task for custom colors and fonts
prompt_templates.prompt: Add Tailwind v4 CSS-first theme tokens for this composition. Use a text/tailwindcss style block and explain the token choices.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Runtime Styles
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task when preview and render differ
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this HyperFrames Tailwind composition for missing style risks. Check runtime readiness, dynamic classes, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production Styling
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced task for stable render pipelines
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess whether this HyperFrames Tailwind project should keep the browser runtime or compile Tailwind to CSS. Include tradeoffs and validation steps.

output_examples.input: A user asks why Tailwind classes appear in preview but not in the exported video.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks Tailwind runtime readiness, looks for dynamic class names, and recommends validation plus a short draft render proof.

output_examples.input: A user wants custom brand colors in a HyperFrames composition.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends Tailwind v4 theme tokens in a browser-runtime style block and avoids adding a Tailwind v3 config file.

output_examples.input: A user asks for animation-friendly Tailwind layout guidance.
output_examples.output: The assistant uses fixed dimensions, static class tokens, transforms, and opacity so rendered frames remain predictable.

best_practices: Use complete static class names for render-critical styles.
Keep Tailwind responsible for layout and static visual style.
Run HyperFrames validation and render a short proof after style changes.
anti_patterns: Do not add tailwind.config.js only to define v4 browser-runtime tokens.
Do not rely on classes assembled only at seek time.
Do not replace the pinned runtime with cdn.tailwindcss.com.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI assistants write and review Tailwind v4 browser-runtime styles for HyperFrames composition HTML.

faq.question: Does it support Tailwind v3 projects?
faq.answer: It mainly covers Tailwind v4 runtime compositions. It warns when v3 setup patterns do not apply.

faq.question: Can it fix missing render styles?
faq.answer: It can guide checks for runtime readiness, static class tokens, and HyperFrames validation steps.

faq.question: Does it run Tailwind automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and command examples, but it does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: When should Tailwind be compiled to CSS?
faq.answer: Compile Tailwind for offline, locked-down, or production-stable renders that should not rely on a browser runtime.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
